sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Osasuna have had mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses, struggling to find consistency. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last 4 games, securing 2 wins and 2 draws, showing improved resilience despite their low league position. Their recent attacking output, led by Iago Aspas, could pose a threat.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, each team has secured a home victory, with Osasuna winning 3-2 in September 2024 and Celta Vigo taking a 1-0 win in February 2025. Matches between these sides tend to be competitive, with goals often scored by both teams, suggesting an open game could be on the cards.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Osasuna, sitting 13th with 10 points, have struggled offensively (7 goals, 19th in the league) but boast a strong defense (2nd fewest goals conceded). Celta Vigo, in 18th with 7 points, have also scored modestly (8 goals) and lack defensive solidity, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 12 matches. Osasuna’s aerial dominance (1st in aerial duels won) could be a key factor.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Osasuna, goalkeeper Sergio Herrera has been in fine form with a 7.56 average Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches, crucial for their defensive stability. Celta Vigo’s Iago Aspas, with 3 goals and an 8.0 average rating recently, remains their talisman and biggest threat going forward.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Considering Osasuna’s home advantage at Estadio El Sadar and their defensive strength, they are slight favorites with Sofascore predicting a 42% chance of a home win. However, Celta Vigo’s recent form and attacking threat suggest they could snatch a point in a likely low-scoring affair. A draw (38% probability) seems a plausible outcome given both teams’ tendencies.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Osasuna leads Celta Vigo by a score of 2-1 in a match that has seen intense action at Estadio El Sadar. Osasuna has dominated much of the first half with superior ball possession and a significant edge in attacking entries. Their two goals, both courtesy of Ante Budimir, highlight their clinical finishing, with one coming from a penalty and the other from open play. Celta Vigo managed to pull one back through Ferran Jutglà, showing they can capitalize on opportunities despite being on the back foot for most of the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Osasuna's dominance
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna has been the stronger side in the first half, controlling the game with a high percentage of possession and numerous entries into the final third and penalty area. Their ability to create chances is evident, with multiple shots on target and a big chance created. Ante Budimir has been the standout performer, netting twice and posing a constant threat upfront. The home side's midfield, with players like Rubén García and Moi Gómez contributing key passes, has been instrumental in maintaining pressure on Celta Vigo's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Celta Vigo's struggle and resilience
halftimeSections.text: Celta Vigo has struggled to match Osasuna's intensity, with significantly less possession and fewer attacking forays into dangerous areas. Their final third entries are limited, and they've failed to create big chances so far. Despite this, they managed to score through Ferran Jutglà, showing a glimpse of their potential on the counter. Defensively, they've been under pressure, conceding multiple shots on target, while disciplinary issues with two yellow cards could hamper their efforts in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with Celta Vigo showing some early intent with brief attacking spells, but Osasuna quickly took control, asserting dominance with consistent attacking momentum for extended periods. The home team enjoyed several prolonged phases of pressure, especially in the middle and latter parts of the half, leading to their two goals. Celta Vigo had occasional moments of threat, managing to break through for their solitary goal, but their attacking spells were far less frequent compared to Osasuna's relentless waves of attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Osasuna appears poised to continue their dominance given their first-half performance and home advantage. They are likely to push for another goal to secure the win, with Ante Budimir being a key figure to watch. Celta Vigo, on the other hand, will need to regroup and find a way to increase their presence in the final third while tightening up defensively to avoid further damage. A more aggressive approach might be necessary for the away side, but they risk leaving gaps at the back against a confident Osasuna side.
